evals.name: watch-stack-from-current-branch
evals.prompt: ok i've got three PRs stacked here — #847 is the bottom (the refactor), #848 adds the new endpoint on top, and #849 is the UI changes. i'm on the top branch (feature/billing-ui) right now. can you keep an eye on the whole stack and rebase the upper ones when stuff merges? the bottom is almost ready to land i think
evals.expected_output: The skill should discover the stack from current branch (#849 → #848 → #847 → main), confirm the chain to the user, start polling all three PRs, and on merge of #847 cascade-rebase #848 and #849.

evals.name: rebase-conflict-during-cascade
evals.prompt: monitor my stack: PRs 412, 413, 414 (412 at bottom). i just merged 412 manually on github. can you rebase 413 and 414 onto main and push them? careful with the lockfile, last time you tried to hand-merge it and broke our CI for like 2 hours
evals.expected_output: The skill should detect #412 merged, rebase #413 onto main, regenerate the lockfile via the package manager instead of merging the conflict by hand, push --force-with-lease, then rebase #414 onto the new #413 head.

evals.name: complex-conflict-escalation
evals.prompt: /loop 3m /babysit-pr-stack 1567 — that's the bottom of a 4-PR stack i'm landing today. watch it, rebase the rest when each one merges, but DON'T touch conflicts in our migration files or anything in deploy/, ping me on those
evals.expected_output: The skill should discover the full 4-PR stack from #1567, run on a 3-minute /loop, cascade-rebase as each merges, auto-resolve safe conflicts, but stop and report to the user with a clear conflict summary when conflicts touch migration files or the deploy/ directory.
